decision: Delta suppression is config-gated via a new [delta] section, field enabled (bool, default true).
reasoning: User chose option C. Keeps parity with delivery-6's [output] pattern (Config gains a field, config.py parses+validates at load). Always-on (option A) rejected as too rigid for a portfolio tool; a bare --full flag (option B) rejected in favor of declarative config consistent with existing [output] section.

decision: Delta membership is computed from a pre-mark_seen snapshot: an item is NEW iff is_seen()==false at the instant BEFORE this run's mark_seen. mark_seen still records ALL collected items unchanged.
reasoning: User chose option A. Correctness-critical. (B) reorder filter-before-mark_seen was REJECTED because it breaks AC-7-019 (mark_seen is decoupled from summarization at collect-time; reordering risks an item being collected but never recorded if it is filtered/fails summarize -> loses idempotency history, the very foundation delta relies on). (C) first_seen_at vs run_started_at REJECTED: _now_utc_z has only second resolution (boundary ambiguity) and would require is_seen to return a timestamp, expanding the adapter API. Option A needs no clock comparison and no State Store Protocol change.
rejected: B: filter before mark_seen (breaks AC-7-019 ordering)
C: compare first_seen_at to run_started_at (second-resolution boundary + API expansion)

decision: When every collected item is already-seen (delta filters all out), the pipeline passes an empty list to render(), which returns the S5 'No new items in the last N days' doc, and delivers it verbatim. Delivery is NOT suppressed.
reasoning: Inferred from AC-7-006 (empty list -> no-new-items doc, exit 0) + delivery AC-6-020 (No-new-items delivered verbatim). Delta only changes WHAT renders, never whether delivery happens.

decision: Delta filter lives in pipeline.py only; StateStore Protocol and JsonFileStateStore public helpers (is_seen/mark_seen) stay unchanged.
reasoning: cross-spec-context §3 constraint 'filter logic lives in pipeline.py only'; state-store ADR-001 keeps Protocol frozen; is_seen already exists and is sufficient.

decision: AC-7-011 (V1: two runs produce byte-identical digest, seen NOT suppressed) is MODIFIED for V2: with delta enabled, a second run over the same issues renders the 'no new items' doc (previously-seen items suppressed).
reasoning: This is the behavioral change V2-001 introduces. AC-7-010 (mark_seen still records all) stays unchanged; only the render/suppression behavior flips.

decision: Delta membership is a pre-mark_seen snapshot: item is NEW iff is_seen()==false BEFORE this run's mark_seen; snapshot captured before any of this run's items are recorded so first-seen-this-run items still appear.
reasoning: Formalizes the A4 decision as a testable business rule referenced by AC-V2-001-001/004/005 and the MODIFIED AC-7-011.

decision: Delta changes only WHICH items are summarized/rendered, never WHICH items are recorded via mark_seen; mark_seen records every collected item regardless of delta_enabled so first_seen_at/idempotency is preserved.
reasoning: Protects AC-7-010 and AC-7-019 from regression; the record-vs-render decoupling is the invariant that makes R1 (ordering) safe.

decision: [delta] enabled validated as strict bool at config-load with type(value) is not bool (bool-trap guard mirroring config.py::_validate_lookback type(value) is not int); non-bool raises ConfigError before run; absent section defaults delta_enabled=true.
reasoning: Watch-item #3 from S1 handoff; isinstance(True,int) trap means isinstance-based checks would accept 1/0 as bool. Mirrors existing [output] fail-fast validation pattern.

decision: Delta is identity-based (repo+item_type+item_id, never content); edited-but-same-id item stays suppressed (EC-005). Filter lives only in pipeline.py, reuses is_seen unchanged, adds no StateStore Protocol method (preserves AC-7-002 boundary).
reasoning: Confirms the EC-005 identity-vs-content trade-off flagged HIGH in S1 active_concerns as an explicit, accepted rule for architect to design against; content-hash re-summarization is summarizer-cache scope, out of this change.

decision: A corrupt/unreadable state file (StateError from load, AC-3-009) surfaces as Error: <msg> exit 1; the run never swallows the error, never treats all items as new, never proceeds with the filter silently disabled.
reasoning: Added to meet R8 3-error-path floor and to close R1's dangerous failure mode: a swallowed StateError could either empty the digest or defeat delta. Reuses the existing StateError contract, no new handling.

decision: mark_seen is called exactly N times (N = collected count) for BOTH delta_enabled=true and false; recorded-item count equals collected-item count, independent of delta_enabled and of how many items the filter suppresses. Only the summarize/render count differs.
reasoning: R1 hardening (user requested stronger guarantee). Converts the ordering risk from a single behavioral test into a count-based invariant that fails if any filter-before-mark_seen reorder skips recording a filtered item. Also drives S3 structural guidance: realize a _partition_new(items, snapshot) that receives a pre-mark_seen snapshot so ordering is enforced by signature, and keep mark_seen(all) unconditional.

decision: Delta filter = module-private _partition_new(items, state) -> (new, seen), called inline in _collect_all right after fetch_items and immediately BEFORE state.mark_seen(items); reads is_seen only. Selection of new-vs-all happens at render_items.extend.
reasoning: Verified is_seen and mark_seen share self._cached, so a filter after mark_seen sees everything as seen (the R1 bug). Inline placement before the existing mark_seen line enforces snapshot-before-write with the smallest diff and keeps AC-7-019 crash-safety ordering unchanged. Guarded by AC-V2-001-010 count test.
rejected: B: full seen-snapshot up front + post-collection filter (more moving parts, duplicates store internals)
C: add is_new/snapshot to StateStore Protocol (violates AC-V2-001-003 frozen Protocol + BR-V2-001-004)

decision: Add _validate_delta(data) in config.py mirroring _validate_lookback; parse [delta] enabled (default true), raise ConfigError when type(value) is not bool (# noqa: E721). Config gains delta_enabled: bool = True.
reasoning: isinstance(True,int) is True, so isinstance would accept 1/0 as bool — bool-trap. type(x) is not bool is the exact guard _validate_lookback already uses for int. Fail-fast at load matches [output] pattern (AC-V2-001-007).
rejected: B: inline in load_config (less testable, [output] block already crowded)
C: coerce 'true'/'false' strings (violates BR-V2-001-003 fail-fast; TOML has native bool)

decision: Add NO new try/except for StateError. Let StateError from is_seen/load propagate through _collect_all (which only catches CollectorError subclasses) to cli.run, which already maps StateError -> Error: <msg> exit 1.
reasoning: Verified StateError(Exception) is not a CollectorError, and cli.py:39 already maps it. Writing defensive code here is the exact anti-pattern AC-V2-001-009 forbids (a mis-scoped except would silently disable the filter). Correctness by omission + a propagation test.
rejected: B: wrap is_seen/_partition_new in try/except StateError and re-raise (redundant; risk of swallowing)

decision: Gate delta_enabled at the render_items.extend site: extend(new if config.delta_enabled else items), using the _partition_new result computed pre-mark_seen. Never re-query is_seen after mark_seen.
reasoning: Keeps 'which is recorded' (all) visibly orthogonal to 'which is rendered' (new/all) at the point of divergence; a post-loop re-filter would read the mutated cache (R1). Render-list is correct by construction, no second pass.
rejected: B: extend all then post-loop re-filter via is_seen (reads mutated cache -> R1 bug)

decision: No openapi.yaml produced for this change; design.md API Design section = N/A.
reasoning: OSS Pulse is a CLI with no HTTP API (project context). The only external surface is the [delta] config key. Analogous to Figma: N/A. Not a rule deviation — R5 requires openapi only when the change has an API.

decision: Added mock_state.is_seen.return_value = False to all 12 pre-existing test_pipeline.py fixtures (bare MagicMock() otherwise returns a truthy MagicMock from is_seen, so _partition_new treats every item as already-seen and V1 tests fail).
reasoning: MagicMock() with no configured return_value is truthy by default; inserting _partition_new before mark_seen means every existing test's mock_state now needs an explicit is_seen contract to preserve its original 'nothing seen yet' V1 semantics. This is the same stub-to-real mocking-boundary class of issue noted in memory/developer.md (scheduler-cli-7).
rejected: leaving fixtures unchanged and special-casing _partition_new to skip when state is a MagicMock (would hide real is_seen bugs)
introducing a shared fixture/conftest helper now (deferred — scope is fixing existing tests, not refactoring test infra)

decision: test_delta_disabled_byte_identical_to_v1 verifies False→False determinism (runs delta_enabled=False twice), but does not explicitly compare a delta_enabled=False run against a delta_enabled=True first-run. The AC-V2-001-006 wording is 'byte-identical to a V1 run' — strictly, V1 = empty prior state + no delta logic, whereas the test sets is_seen=True (all seen). The code review confirms the single extend() conditional makes this a non-issue in practice, and test_delta_mark_seen_count_invariant_both_modes already exercises both modes over the same items.
reasoning: S4 test authoring — test proves determinism but not cross-mode identity. The combination of the count invariant test + code review is sufficient coverage; this is a test-improvement opportunity, not a blocking defect. [AI-DETECTABLE] — reviewable at PR time.
rejected: blocking GO — insufficient since code review + count invariant test fill the gap
